name: meshy-mcp
description: Expose Meshy.ai Text-to-3D APIs to GitHub Copilot as language model tools

meshy.apiKey.markdownDescription: Your Meshy.ai API key. You can also set the `MESHY_API_KEY` environment variable.

meshy.pollIntervalSeconds.description: How often (in seconds) to poll Meshy for task status.

meshy.defaultStyleSuffix.description: Style description appended to every prompt (e.g. 'low-poly game asset, PBR textures').

meshy.defaultNegativePrompt.description: Shared negative prompt applied to every generation.

languageModelTools.name: meshy_preview
languageModelTools.userDescription: Submit a Text-to-3D preview task to Meshy.ai.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Submit a Text-to-3D preview task to Meshy.ai. Returns a task ID that you can pass to meshy_status to check progress, or meshy_refine to add PBR textures. Cost: 20 credits.

prompt.description: Description of the 3D asset to generate

style_suffix.description: Optional style text appended to the prompt (e.g. 'low-poly game asset')

negative_prompt.description: What to avoid in the generation

image_url.description: Optional reference image URL to guide the generation (image-to-3D mode)

topology.description: Mesh topology. Use 'quad' for game assets (default: quad)

languageModelTools.name: meshy_refine
languageModelTools.userDescription: Add PBR textures to a completed Meshy preview task.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Submit a refine task for a completed Meshy preview. Adds high-quality PBR textures (albedo, normal, roughness, metallic). Returns a new task ID. Cost: 10 credits.

preview_task_id.description: The task ID returned by meshy_preview (must have status SUCCEEDED)

languageModelTools.name: meshy_status
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check the status and progress of a Meshy.ai task.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the current status and progress of a Meshy.ai task. Returns status (PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCEEDED, FAILED), progress percentage, preceding_tasks count, and model_urls when complete.

task_id.description: The Meshy task ID to check

languageModelTools.name: meshy_download
languageModelTools.userDescription: Download the GLB file from a completed Meshy task to a local path.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Download the GLB file from a completed Meshy task to a local path. The task must have status SUCCEEDED.

task_id.description: The completed Meshy task ID (preview or refine)

output_path.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path to write the .glb file (e.g. Assets/Models/my_asset.glb)

languageModelTools.name: meshy_generate
languageModelTools.userDescription: Generate a single 3D asset end-to-end via Meshy.ai (preview → refine → download).
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run the full Meshy.ai pipeline end-to-end: submit preview → poll until done → submit refine → poll until done → download GLB. PREFER THIS TOOL over calling meshy_preview, meshy_refine, meshy_status, and meshy_download individually whenever the user wants to generate a single 3D asset from a text description. Only use the individual tools if the user explicitly asks for step-by-step control. Total cost: 30 credits (or 20 credits if skip_refine is true).

prompt.description: Description of the 3D asset to generate

output_path.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path to write the .glb file

style_suffix.description: Style text appended to the prompt

negative_prompt.description: What to avoid in the generation

skip_refine.description: Skip refine stage (faster, no PBR, costs only 20 credits)

languageModelTools.name: meshy_generate_from_file
languageModelTools.userDescription: Batch-generate 3D assets from a JSON assets definition file via Meshy.ai.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Process a JSON assets definition file and generate all pending 3D assets via Meshy.ai. PREFER THIS TOOL over meshy_generate when the user mentions a file, a list of assets, multiple models, or a batch job. The JSON file has a 'settings' block (shared style_suffix and negative_prompt) and an 'assets' array where each entry has 'id', 'prompt', and 'output_path'. Progress is written back to the file after each step so interrupted runs resume automatically from where they left off. Use 'only' to filter to specific asset IDs, 'dry_run' to preview without calling the API.

assets_file.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path to the assets JSON file

only.description: Only process assets whose 'id' field contains at least one of these strings

skip_refine.description: Skip the refine stage for all assets

dry_run.description: Print what would be generated without calling the API
